onboarding.note: Blank Zap plus draft saving gives a concrete first-run path.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS needs equally low-friction first setup without exposing runtime mechanics.

create_automation.note: Creation starts directly in the Zap editor with trigger then action flow.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS can stay simpler only if the first automation path remains shorter and safer.

preview_simulation.note: Official test mode evidence is connector specific, not a platform-wide deterministic preview.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS still has room to differentiate with a single preview contract instead of app-by-app testing.

approval.note: Human in the Loop can pause runs for review and approval.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS must keep its approval flow more contextual, not merely equivalent in pause mechanics.

risk_explanation.note: Approval mechanics are documented, but risk language is not.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS should treat explicit risk explanation as a real trust-core differentiator.

evidence_receipt.note: Zap history exposes run details, filters and usage review.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS receipts need to be as traceable while staying more human-readable.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: Replay exists, but the source does not claim honest undo of external effects.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS should keep the reversible versus compensating distinction explicit.

failure_degraded_mode.note: Run history and statuses provide a strong failure inspection baseline.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should match the inspectability while giving clearer next-step guidance.

mobile.note: No current first-party mobile control source was verified.
mobile.aris_implication: Do not assume Zapier beats ARIS on mobile until an official current source exists.

auditability.note: History supports search, filters and run detail review.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS needs equivalent traceability with less operator jargon.

dashboard.note: Task usage and run views exist, but the packet did not verify an advanced operational dashboard narrative.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS can still frame dashboard value around actionability instead of raw usage.

demo.note: Connector-level test mode supports safe demoing without real charges.
demo.aris_implication: ARIS should preserve the same no-real-money clarity in its sandbox stories.

onboarding.note: The first-scenario tutorial gives a concrete builder path.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS needs equally explicit setup guidance without node-graph overload.

create_automation.note: Scenario creation and management are explicit in both tutorial and API language.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS should keep advanced capability hidden behind a safer business-first layer.

preview_simulation.note: The verified sources show creation and execution history, not deterministic preview.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS preview remains a meaningful gap-closing opportunity.

approval.note: No official approval gate strong enough for scoring was verified.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS should avoid assuming parity where Make source evidence is absent.

risk_explanation.note: No official source in this packet proved user-facing risk explanation.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS can turn risk explanation into a product-level contrast, not a hidden backend concern.

evidence_receipt.note: Scenario history and CSV export exist, but not a customer-facing receipt model.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS receipts should feel less like telemetry export and more like proof.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: History mentions replay details, but not honest business rollback semantics.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS should keep rollback language explicit and bounded.

failure_degraded_mode.note: Operational logs are present, but a degraded-state UX was not evidenced.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should translate failure inspection into clearer next-step guidance.

mobile.note: No current official mobile control document was verified.
mobile.aris_implication: ARIS can claim mobile intent only after its own future proof, but Make has no verified advantage here.

auditability.note: Run history, change log and export support operator audit work.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS should meet this level of inspectability with less expert framing.

dashboard.note: Per-scenario history is strong, but a broader action dashboard was not verified.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS dashboard should optimize for decision triage, not just run inspection.

demo.note: Tutorial gives a demo-like starter story, but not a bounded safe sandbox script.
demo.aris_implication: ARIS can outperform here if BENCHUIX-26 stays short and trust-centered.

onboarding.note: Cloud is quick to start, but self-hosted onboarding explicitly requires technical expertise.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS should preserve owner-solo simplicity while keeping advanced mode optional.

create_automation.note: Workflow canvas creation is explicit and fully documented.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS can learn from power-user capability without inheriting the same complexity baseline.

preview_simulation.note: Manual executions while inactive create a strong test-before-publish pattern.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS preview should stay more business-readable than a raw execution test.

approval.note: Human approval can gate tool execution before the agent acts.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS should retain approval strength while reducing AI-builder jargon.

risk_explanation.note: Approval exists, but the verified source does not prove owner-facing risk explanation.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS should keep its risk microcopy advantage explicit.

evidence_receipt.note: Executions are inspectable, but the packet did not verify receipt-style proof for nontechnical users.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS should keep evidence readable without losing traceability.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: Workflow export/import helps change control, not business-effect compensation.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS rollback semantics should stay tied to impact honesty, not just artifact portability.

failure_degraded_mode.note: Execution inspection exists, but degraded-state UX for business operators was not evidenced.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should keep failure states human-legible.

mobile.note: No official mobile control surface was verified.
mobile.aris_implication: Do not score mobile advantage against n8n without a first-party source.

auditability.note: Execution history is strong for builders, but user-facing audit framing is not proven here.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS should bridge traceability and business comprehension.

dashboard.note: Evaluations and quality tracking exist, but not a verified owner-solo operational dashboard.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS can learn from evaluation rigor without copying a builder-first surface.

demo.note: Manual test runs provide a concrete demo path before publishing.
demo.aris_implication: ARIS demo should remain shorter and more trust-focused than builder-led walkthroughs.

onboarding.note: The platform overview proves broad flow categories, but not a fast owner-solo path.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS should stay lighter than enterprise-first onboarding.

create_automation.note: The approval workflow doc proves explicit flow construction with approval actions.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS should match clarity while avoiding admin-center complexity.

preview_simulation.note: Run history and monitoring are strong, but deterministic preview before execution was not evidenced.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS preview can still be differentiated as a pre-execution trust contract.

approval.note: Approval flow support is explicit and mature.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS should keep approval simpler and more decision-oriented, not just parity in routing.

risk_explanation.note: Approval actions are documented, but rich risk explanation language is not.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS can compete on explanation quality instead of workflow breadth.

evidence_receipt.note: FlowRun records expose status, duration, trigger type and errors at scale.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS should decide how much of this depth becomes customer-visible proof.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: Rich run records exist, but no honest undo model was verified.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS should keep rollback semantics more explicit than enterprise telemetry.

failure_degraded_mode.note: Run records and automation center monitoring create a strong troubleshooting baseline.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should emulate the monitoring depth only where it improves human decisions.

mobile.note: Mobile approve or reject with push notification is explicitly documented.
mobile.aris_implication: ARIS mobile companion should keep the same actionability with less enterprise framing.

auditability.note: Purview logs cover flow lifecycle and permission changes.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS needs a future runtime-grade audit layer if it wants to match enterprise governance depth.

dashboard.note: Admin analytics cover runs, usage, errors and flow types.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS should define whether its dashboard targets owner action or admin reporting first.

demo.note: The docs make tutorial-style demos possible, but not a bounded sandbox story optimized for trust.
demo.aris_implication: BENCHUIX-26 can still differentiate with a shorter narrative.

onboarding.note: Automation setup is documented and AI can assist in creation.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS should keep its first automation path at least this direct while remaining safer.

create_automation.note: Official AI actions and examples prove automation creation support.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS should learn from guided creation without drifting into app-builder complexity.

preview_simulation.note: History and revision are strong, but a deterministic preview was not verified.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS preview remains an important trust differentiator.

approval.note: Official mobile interfaces include approve or reject actions.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS approvals should keep the same immediacy with richer risk context.

risk_explanation.note: Action support exists, but risk explanation language is not evidenced.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS should preserve risk literacy as part of the task, not an optional extra.

evidence_receipt.note: Automation history and version history exist, but not a dedicated business receipt model.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS should make evidence feel more explicit and user-readable.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: Version history helps workflow rollback, but external-effect compensation is not proven.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS should keep rollback honesty tied to effect type.

failure_degraded_mode.note: History aids troubleshooting, but degraded UX guidance was not verified.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should make failure states more directive than mere run history.

mobile.note: Mobile-optimized interfaces and immediate actions are explicitly documented.
mobile.aris_implication: ARIS mobile work must stay owner-first, not just responsive.

auditability.note: Audit log search, details and API support are explicit.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS will need a runtime-grade audit plan later if it wants comparable governance depth.

dashboard.note: The verified source proves admin reports and logs, not an advanced operator dashboard.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS can make dashboard actionability a clearer product story than admin reporting.

demo.note: AI creation examples support demoability, but not a strict trust-first sandbox narrative.
demo.aris_implication: ARIS should keep demo framing around safe decisions and proof.

onboarding.note: The overview proves breadth, but not a short owner-solo path.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS should not copy CRM-platform sprawl into the first-run flow.

create_automation.note: Official workflow action can deploy agents directly from workflows.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS should learn from workflow-integrated AI while keeping the control plane simpler.

preview_simulation.note: The verified sources prove workflow and approval capabilities, not deterministic preview.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS preview remains a strategic opportunity.

approval.note: Approvals cover records, content, exports, quotes and marketing assets.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS should keep approval breadth secondary to clarity and safety.

risk_explanation.note: Approval availability is explicit, but risk explanation quality is not evidenced.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS can differentiate through stronger explanation of impact and limits.

evidence_receipt.note: Audit logs and summaries are strong, but a customer-facing receipt model was not verified.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS should decide how to expose proof without forcing audit-log literacy.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: Workflow changes can be reviewed and reverted, but effect compensation is not evidenced.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS should keep rollback semantics tied to business consequence, not only config history.

failure_degraded_mode.note: Activity history and summaries help review actions, but degraded-state UX was not directly evidenced.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should render failure states more explicitly than audit-centric tooling.

mobile.note: Official mobile approval and request-change flows are explicit.
mobile.aris_implication: ARIS mobile companion should keep actionability but use calmer decision language.

auditability.note: Audit logs, AI summary and employee access history provide strong governance evidence.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS needs a later runtime path if it wants comparable trace depth.

dashboard.note: Analyze tab totals and charts create a real operational reporting surface.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS dashboard should be more action-centered than log-centered.

demo.note: Breadth supports demo packaging, but the packet did not verify a bounded trust-first sandbox story.
demo.aris_implication: ARIS can still win on short, scenario-based safe demos.

onboarding.note: The official page proves lifecycle tooling, but it is enterprise and marketing heavy.
onboarding.aris_implication: ARIS should avoid inheriting enterprise-scale overhead into first contact.

create_automation.note: Natural-language configuration plus build/test/supervise language indicates broad creation power.
create_automation.aris_implication: ARIS can stay narrower but clearer, especially for owner-solo automation creation.

preview_simulation.note: Testing Center is described as a sandbox-like environment for simulated interactions.
preview_simulation.aris_implication: ARIS should preserve a more deterministic and shorter preview story in BENCHUIX-26.

approval.note: Complex multi-step approval rules are explicitly available.
approval.aris_implication: ARIS should not mimic complexity by default; it should expose only the necessary gate.

risk_explanation.note: Event logs help testing and troubleshooting, but owner-facing risk explanation is not proven.
risk_explanation.aris_implication: ARIS can still differentiate on human-readable risk and impact language.

evidence_receipt.note: Event logs and audit data exist, but no customer receipt model was verified.
evidence_receipt.aris_implication: ARIS should decide when deep telemetry becomes visible proof.

rollback_undo_compensate.note: The verified sources do not prove an honest undo or compensation surface.
rollback_undo_compensate.aris_implication: ARIS should keep rollback honesty as an explicit product choice.

failure_degraded_mode.note: Logs and supervision imply strong troubleshooting, but degraded operator UX was not verified.
failure_degraded_mode.aris_implication: ARIS should render degraded states as decisions, not just traces.

mobile.note: Official help snippets confirm deployment to Salesforce mobile surfaces.
mobile.aris_implication: ARIS should keep mobile narrow, fast and decision-safe rather than broad.

auditability.note: Enhanced event logs and audit artifacts create a strong trace baseline.
auditability.aris_implication: ARIS will need a runtime-grade audit layer later if it wants enterprise parity.

dashboard.note: Official snippets confirm audit and feedback reports and dashboards.
dashboard.aris_implication: ARIS should anchor dashboard value in actionability, not only oversight.

demo.note: Testing Center provides a strong official demo and test story.
demo.aris_implication: ARIS demo must stay simpler and clearer to avoid looking unfinished beside enterprise tooling.
